intro_text: The hunting patrol spots a juvenile eagle on the wing over a large bend in the river, and watches as it swoops to dig its talons through the spine of an impressively big fish. But then it doesn't take its catch into the air. Perhaps it's too heavy?
decline_text: They move on, ignoring the eagle to focus on their own hunt.

success_outcomes.text: The patrol uses the eagle as a scout for their own hunt, and eventually manage several smaller catches from the same riverbank that cumulatively are just as impressive as the eagle's massive catch.

log.cats_from: cat_from and cat_to used an eagle as a scout on a hunting patrol and found success.

success_outcomes.text: The cats' eyes light up with possibility, watching the eagle swimming awkwardly. p_l leads the patrol yowling threats and insults from the riverbank and making a massive racket. Bewildered, the eagle eventually gives up its catch and takes to the air. The massive fish is c_n's as soon as they can work out how to fish it from the river.

log.cats_from: cat_from and cat_to worked together to scare an eagle off and steal its prey.

success_outcomes.text: As the rest of the patrol discuss whether they can steal the catch, s_c fixates on the bird that caught it. The eagle works its way awkwardly towards the bank. Just as it's going to climb out, s_c pounces, fighting in the shallows where s_c has the edge over the bird. {PRONOUN/s_c/subject/CAP} {VERB/s_c/win/wins} both the fish and the predator as prey for c_n.

log.cats_from: cat_from was amazed that cat_to brought down both an juvenile eagle and its prey on a hunting patrol.
cat_from saw cat_to's hunting patrol return with both an juvenile eagle and a big fish for the fresh-kill pile.

fail_outcomes.text: The patrol dives into the water, keen to make a play to steal the fish, but even though its talons are occupied keeping grip on its prey, the eagle's huge wings pack a buffeting punch and they're driven back, giving up on the idea of scavenging.

log.cats_from: cat_from and cat_to failed to steal an eagle's prey on a hunt.

intro_text: It starts snowing soon after the patrol sets out, trying to bring back something for the fresh-kill pile.
decline_text: They decide to turn back and wait until the snow dies down.

intro_text: As the patrol walks through the wooded slopes, a loud chittering from above interrupts them. r_c looks up just in time to get a shower of twigs and dead leaves to the face, while p_l exclaims aloud as {PRONOUN/p_l/subject} {VERB/p_l/spot/spots} squirrels darting through the branches above.

success_outcomes.text: Before anyone can even speak, there's a loud crack from above. The cats scatter as a branch crashes down, bringing both squirrels and a shower of oak leaves with it! The patrol thinks fast and pounces on the squirrels before they can escape. They head home with their bounty, each cat feeling quite pleased with themself.

intro_text: As the days grow shorter and the nights grow colder, p_l leads a patrol down to the foothills to partake in one of c_n's favorite annual hunting traditions: the salmon run.
decline_text: When {PRONOUN/p_l/subject} {VERB/p_l/step/steps} out of camp, p_l sees the storm brewing on the horizon and thinks better of it.

success_outcomes.text: p_l crouches on one slick rock, almost nose-to-nose with the rushing rapids below. r_c takes the other side of the stream. Both cats narrow their eyes in concentration, ready, ready... p_l strikes as a salmon flips up out of the water, batting it to r_c, who kills it neatly. They continue all afternoon and bring home a sizeable haul.

success_outcomes.text: p_l leads the patrol to a fast-flowing stream, and the cats position themselves along the bank. With enough cats to react to any salmon that pops out of the current, the patrol returns to camp with mouths full of fish.

intro_text: As the wind scatters the orange leaf-pelts of the trees, pipistrelle bats arrive en masse to court, males belting out their high-pitched songs of love.

success_outcomes.text: Distracted by love and the competition of performances to win it, the males make easy hunting for their delighted audience of c_n cats.
